ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Go to the [Bible Hub Complete Biblical Timeline](https://biblehub.com/timeline/) to find dates to put in your Book of Centuries.
[ ] Listen to [Psalms](https://thepsalmsproject.com/music) set to music, or create your own music to accompany one of the Psalms you read this term. (You could use piano, rhythm sticks, tamborine, drum, etc.)

[ ] Explore the virtual resources at the [Museum of the Bible](https://www.museumofthebible.org/museum-from-home). (Note: a teacher should preview.)
[ ] Write a favorite Psalm on watercolor paper and then decorate the border with simple brush drawings. You can frame the finished art to hang in your home or give it as a gift.

[ ] [This website](https://www.opendoorsuk.org/persecution/world-watch-list/) lists the countries where Christians are still persecuted today and how we can pray for them.
[ ] Share a meal with others such as taking donations to a food bank, taking a meal to a neighbor, or baking cookies for a local fire or police station.
